concepts.title: Electromagnetic Waves are Transverse
concepts.description: Electromagnetic waves propagate through a medium in a transverse direction, i.e., the oscillations are perpendicular to the direction of propagation. This is a fundamental property of electromagnetic waves.

concepts.title: Electromagnetic Spectrum
concepts.description: The electromagnetic spectrum includes all types of electromagnetic waves, ranging from low-frequency, long-wavelength waves (like radio waves) to high-frequency, short-wavelength waves (like gamma rays).

concepts.title: Speed of Light in Vacuum
concepts.description: The speed of light in vacuum is a constant (approximately 3 x 10^8 m/s) and is a fundamental physical constant. This value is crucial in various electromagnetic wave-related calculations.

concepts.title: Electromagnetic Wave Types
concepts.description: Use the acronym ROYGBIV to remember the order of electromagnetic waves in increasing order of wavelength: Radio waves, Ohmic waves (Microwaves), Yellow (Visible Light), Green (Green Light), Blue (Blue Light), Indigo (Indigo Light), Violet (Violet Light).

concepts.title: Confusion between Speed and Frequency
concepts.description: Students often mix up the speed and frequency of electromagnetic waves. Remember that the speed of light in vacuum is a constant, while the frequency of electromagnetic waves depends on the source and the type of wave.

concepts.title: Units of Energy and Frequency
concepts.description: Recall that the unit of energy for electromagnetic waves is the Joule (J) and the unit of frequency is Hertz (Hz). These units are essential for calculations involving electromagnetic waves.

concepts.title: Electromagnetic Waves are Polarized
concepts.description: Electromagnetic waves can be polarized, meaning the orientation of the electric field vector can be controlled and manipulated. This property is crucial in various applications, including communication and optics.

concepts.title: Interference and Diffraction
concepts.description: Electromagnetic waves exhibit both interference and diffraction phenomena, which are essential characteristics of wave behavior. These phenomena are important in understanding various electromagnetic wave-related phenomena.
